This was chosen because of its high practical relevance and widespread use in many electrochemical systems. For instance, commercially available catalysts for fuel cell systems typically consist of Pt NPs supported on high\u2013surface area carbon black (Pt/C) ( 32, 33).", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": ["For this purpose, we prepared GDEs by sputtering 200 nm of Cu onto porous hydrophobic polytetrafluoroethylene (PTFE) substrates (Cu PTFE).", "Next, we spray coated the F-doped carbon black onto this with a loading of 1 mg cm \u22122 to form a composite catalyst which we termed as Cu PTFE/C-F.", "We observed that the thickness of the Cu layer is ~200 nm, while the C-F layer atop the Cu is ~4.3 \u03bcm.", "As a control case, we also spray coated undoped carbon black with a similar loading of 1 mg cm \u22122 onto Cu PTFE to yield Cu PTFE/C.", "For instance, at 400 mA cm \u22122, the C 2+ FE attains a maximum value of 82.5% with a partial current density of 330 mA cm \u22122 for Cu PTFE/C-F ( Fig. 4A and tables S20 and S21)."], "knowledge_contribution": "Evidence: For this purpose, we prepared GDEs by sputtering 200 nm of Cu onto porous hydrophobic polytetrafluoroethylene (PTFE) sub"}, {"url": "https://www.frontiersin.org/journals/chemistry/articles/10.3389/fchem.2022.931767/full", "title": "Electrochemical Reduction of CO 2 With Good Efficiency ...", "summary": "Abstract Carbon monoxide (CO) and formic acid (HCOOH) are suggested to be the most convenient products from electrochemical reduction of CO2 according to techno-economic analysis. To date, tremendous advances have been achieved in the development of catalysts and processes, which make this research topic even more interesting to both academic and industrial sectors. In this work, we report nanostructured Cu-Al materials that are able to convert CO2 to CO and HCOOH with good efficiency.", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": ["Good current densities of 67 and 130 mA cm\u22122 are obtained at \u22121.0 V and \u22121.3 V vs. reversible hydrogen electrode (RHE), respectively.", "When switching the electrolyte to KOH, a significant selectivity up to 20% is observed for C2H4 formation, and the current densities achieve 146 and 222 mA cm\u22122 at \u22121.0 V and \u22121.3 V vs.", "Despite significant prospect, the electrochemical CO2 reduction reaction (CO2RR) encounters many challenges associated to high energy barriers, multiple parallel reactions and competitive hydrogen evolution reaction (HER) (Yin et al., 2019).", "Among these species, CO and its mixture with H2 (H2/CO syngas) have high relevance for the chemical industry (Nielsen et al., 2018; Zeng et al., 2020a).", "Particularly, several processes were already well-established for the generation of fuels and fine-chemicals from syngas (Hern\u00e1ndez et al., 2017)."], "knowledge_contribution": "Evidence: Good current densities of 67 and 130 mA cm\u22122 are obtained at \u22121.0 V and \u22121.3 V vs. reversible hydrogen electrode (RHE), "}, {"url": "https://www.mdpi.com/1420-3049/28/20/7016", "title": "Development of Different Kinds of Electrocatalyst for the ...", "summary": "Development of Different Kinds of Electrocatalyst for the Electrochemical Reduction of Carbon Dioxide Reactions: An Overview Abstract 1.
